nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: CONDÉ
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: TRITH
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: apparently from Old High German tretan ‘to step’. However, a South German family of this name are descendants of late 16th-century immigrants from Como, Italy, who bore the name de Triddi.

variants.name: Treat
meanings: Americanized form of German   Tritt   or   Trede  .
English: variant of   Threatt  .

nameMeanings.name: Basilie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Domingo, Roberto, Carlos, Ricardo, Albertino, Alejandro, Almario, Altagracia, Andres, Armando.  Portuguese Ilidio.  Italian Antonio, Gennaro, Anastasio, Federico, Marco, Quirino.
Italian; Portuguese (Basílio): from the personal name Basilio, Basílio, from Latin Basilius (see Basil ).

variants.name: Basil
meanings: From the Greek name Basileios ‘royal’ (a derivative of basileus ‘king’). This name was borne by St Basil the Great ( c.330–379 ), bishop of Caesarea, a theologian regarded as one of the Fathers of the Eastern Church. It was also the name of several early saints martyred in the East.

variants.name: Bacilio
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Peru): variant spelling of   Basilio  .

en.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH was born in 1169, in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. She had at least 1 daughter with Wauthier IV de Fontaines.

fr.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH est née en 1169, à Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Wauthier IV de Fontaines.

de.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH wurde 1169 in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren. Sie hatte mit Wauthier IV de Fontaines mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH è nata il nel 1169 nel Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Wauthier IV de Fontaines.

ko.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH(은)는 1169년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Wauthier IV de Fontaines(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH nasceu em 1169, em Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Wauthier IV de Fontaines.

ru.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH родилась в 1169 г. в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Wauthier IV de Fontaines.

es.lifeSummary: Basilie DE CONDÉ DE TRITH nació en 1169, en Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Wauthier IV de Fontaines.
